old: Every year on New Year's Eve we eat the Chinese New Year's Eve family dinner.
new: Every year on New Year's Eve, we eat the reunion dinner.
reason: Redundant repetition of 'New Year's Eve' is awkward; 'reunion dinner' is the standard English rendering of 年夜饭
合适 does not take a personal object in natural Chinese; 适合 + person is the correct collocation

old: This pair of jeans suits you well.
new: This pair of jeans suits you very well.
reason: Translation adjusted to match corrected sentence (very)

new: 他们属于不同的政治派别。
reason: 政治派 is not a natural standalone compound; 政治派别 is the standard term; 属于 is the natural verb here

old: They belong to different political schools of thought.
new: They belong to different political factions.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence; 派别 is more naturally rendered as 'factions'

old: 春天是很多动物配的季节。
new: 春天是很多动物交配的季节。
reason: 配 alone for 'mate' is too terse and unnatural; 交配 is the standard word for animal mating

old: Spring is the season when many animals mate.
new: Spring is the mating season for many animals.
reason: Smoother English phrasing to match corrected sentence

new: 他平了全国纪录，让大家震惊。
reason: Definition is 'to match (a record)'; old sentence illustrates a draw/tie, not matching a record — definition-sentence mismatch

old: After the match ended, it was a draw.
new: He matched the national record, astonishing everyone.
reason: Translation updated to match corrected sentence

old: 工作欠经验，需要多学习。
new: 他工作时欠经验，还需要多学习。
reason: Original sentence lacks a subject, making it grammatically awkward; adding 他 and 时 makes it natural

old: The work lacks experience; more learning is needed.
new: He lacks experience at work and still needs to learn more.
reason: Translation updated to match corrected sentence

old: He is abroad and misses his close relatives and friends very much.
new: He is abroad and misses his close relatives very much.
reason: 亲人 means close family relatives only, not friends; 'and friends' is an unsupported addition

old: All her close relatives and friends came to the wedding.
new: All her close relatives came to the wedding.
reason: 亲人 means close family relatives only, not friends; 'and friends' is an unsupported addition
